name: delete-relationship
description: Delete an entity-to-entity relationship from the knowledge graph. Identified by subject name, predicate (edge type), and object name. Permanent — cannot be undone. Use only when the user explicitly says a relationship is wrong or should be removed.

inputs.subject: string — name/label of the source entity (e.g. 'Joseph Fung')
inputs.predicate: string — the edge type to delete (e.g. 'spouse', 'reports_to'). Must be a known edge type.
inputs.object: string — name/label of the target entity (e.g. 'Xiaopu Fung')

outputs.deleted: boolean — true if a matching edge was found and removed
outputs.edge_id: string? — the ID of the deleted edge (populated when deleted is true)
outputs.ambiguous: boolean? — true when subject or object matched multiple nodes
outputs.candidates: array? — [{id, label, type}] for the ambiguous entity, when ambiguous is true
outputs.ambiguous_field: string? — 'subject' or 'object', indicating which was ambiguous
